original_hypothesis.title: Flawed Zero Address Check in setInternalBribe Can Break Fee Claims
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: The `setInternalBribe` function uses a tautological check `require(_int >= address(0))` which always passes. This allows the admin to accidentally set the internal bribe to the zero address, breaking LP fee distribution.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: In Solidity, all addresses are represented as `uint160`, so any address is `>= address(0)`. If the owner sets `internal_bribe` to `address(0)`, the `claimFees` function will attempt to call `notifyRewardAmount` on the zero address, which will revert, preventing anyone from claiming fees.
preconditions: Owner accidentally or maliciously sets internal_bribe to address(0)

initial_code_evidence.specific_concern: Allows setting critical contract dependency to zero address

analysis_questions: Does the factory ever deploy gauges with address(0) as the internal bribe?

original_hypothesis.preliminary_attack_scenario: The owner makes a configuration mistake and sets the internal bribe to `address(0)`. When the gauge attempts to distribute accumulated fees via `claimFees`, the transaction reverts, locking the fees until the owner fixes the configuration.

potential_impact_if_confirmed.recovery_possibility: Owner can fix by setting a valid address

original_hypothesis.test_coverage_gap: Missing negative tests for zero address validation

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
